title: Lesson 169: (Grammar) - than; from; out of; since; at; on; except; but; ot...

intro_cards.title: Comparison Particle: より
intro_cards.body:
より on its own means 'than' in comparisons, or 'from' in formal/written Japanese.

1. Comparison ('than'):
• 猫より犬が好きです。(I prefer dogs to cats.)
• 昨日より今日のほうが暖かい。
(Today is warmer than yesterday.)

2. Starting point (formal/written only):
• 午後3時より開始します。(Starts from 3 PM.)
• 東京より参りました。(I've come from Tokyo.)

Note: In everyday speech, use から for 'from.'
より as 'from' appears only in formal written announcements.
